metadata.description: Resource name prefix. To deploy side-by-side in the same RG, MUST differ from the old stack's prefix (or use a different `environment`).
Environment tier. Combined with namingPrefix to differentiate the new stack from the old. Common pattern: old = prod, new = prd2.
Primary Azure region for all infra resources. Should match the old stack to keep latency-sensitive user data local.
AVD control-plane metadata location. MUST match the old stack so users keep a single MSAL/feed surface.
Tags applied to every resource. Merged with stack-generated tags.
CIDR for the NEW AVD spoke VNet. Must NOT overlap the old stack's VNet if peering or shared transit.
CIDR for session-host subnet. Must be inside vnetAddressSpace.
CIDR for private-endpoint subnet.
CIDR for optional management/jumpbox subnet. Pass empty string to skip.
Resource ID of an existing hub VNet to peer with. Empty = standalone spoke.
Custom DNS server IPs. Empty = Azure-provided DNS (recommended with Private DNS zones).
Pooled = multi-session shared hosts. Personal = 1:1 user-to-host.
Connection load-balancing algorithm. Persistent applies to Personal only.
Max concurrent user sessions per Pooled host.
Preferred app group type for this host pool.
If true, host pool receives AVD service updates first (canary).
Auto-start a stopped host when a user connects.
Session-host VM SKU.
Number of session hosts to provision in the NEW stack.
Availability zones to spread VMs across. Empty array = no zone.
OS disk storage SKU.
OS disk size in GiB.
Image to deploy. Two shapes: {kind:"marketplace", alias:"<key>"} OR {kind:"customImage", resourceId:"/subscriptions/..."}.
Local admin username on session hosts.
Local admin password. Source from Key Vault in bicepparam files; never commit.
Enroll Entra-joined hosts into Intune (MDM).

Entra security group object ID that receives "Desktop Virtualization User" on the NEW app group AND "Virtual Machine User Login" on NEW session hosts. Usually the SAME group as the old stack so users have access to both during cutover.
Entra security group for "Virtual Machine Administrator Login" on session hosts. Empty = skip.
Managed = UDP 3390 inbound. Public = STUN/TURN UDP 3478/3479. Both = enable both. None = disabled.
If true, deploy NEW Azure Files (Entra Kerberos) + PE for FSLogix. Profiles do NOT auto-migrate from the old share — see runbook for FSLogix migration guidance.
Storage account SKU for FSLogix file share.
Premium file share quota in GiB.
BYO Log Analytics workspace resource ID. Empty = create a new workspace. To keep one observability surface across old + new, pass the OLD stack's LAW ID here.
Log retention for the new workspace (ignored if BYO).

[cutover only] Resource ID of the EXISTING host pool being replaced. Used for validation + drain runbook snippet emission. Format: /subscriptions/<subId>/resourceGroups/<rg>/providers/Microsoft.DesktopVirtualization/hostPools/<name>
[cutover only] Resource ID of the EXISTING workspace being retired. Format: /subscriptions/<subId>/resourceGroups/<rg>/providers/Microsoft.DesktopVirtualization/workspaces/<name>
[cutover only] If true, the deployment OUTPUT includes the drain PowerShell snippet for the OPERATOR to run. We do not mutate the old host pool from Bicep — a PUT would overwrite every property.

properties.securityRules: [concat(createArray(createObject('name', 'Allow-AVD-Reverse-Connect-Out', 'properties', createObject('description', 'Session hosts reach AVD broker via outbound 443 over WindowsVirtualDesktop service tag (reverse connect).', 'priority', 100, 'direction', 'Outbound', 'access', 'Allow', 'protocol', 'Tcp', 'sourceAddressPrefix', '*', 'sourcePortRange', '*', 'destinationAddressPrefix', 'WindowsVirtualDesktop', 'destinationPortRange', '443')), createObject('name', 'Allow-AzureCloud-Out-443', 'properties', createObject('description', 'Outbound 443 to AzureCloud service tag for management, Storage, Monitor, KeyVault, etc.', 'priority', 110, 'direction', 'Outbound', 'access', 'Allow', 'protocol', 'Tcp', 'sourceAddressPrefix', '*', 'sourcePortRange', '*', 'destinationAddressPrefix', 'AzureCloud', 'destinationPortRange', '443')), createObject('name', 'Allow-AzureActiveDirectory-Out', 'properties', createObject('description', 'Outbound to Entra ID for join, sign-in, token refresh.', 'priority', 120, 'direction', 'Outbound', 'access', 'Allow', 'protocol', 'Tcp', 'sourceAddressPrefix', '*', 'sourcePortRange', '*', 'destinationAddressPrefix', 'AzureActiveDirectory', 'destinationPortRange', '443'))), if(variables('allowShortpathInbound'), createArray(createObject('name', 'Allow-Shortpath-UDP-3390-In', 'properties', createObject('description', 'RDP Shortpath Managed networks: UDP 3390 inbound from intranet (VirtualNetwork tag).', 'priority', 200, 'direction', 'Inbound', 'access', 'Allow', 'protocol', 'Udp', 'sourceAddressPrefix', 'VirtualNetwork', 'sourcePortRange', '*', 'destinationAddressPrefix', '*', 'destinationPortRange', '3390'))), createArray()), if(variables('allowShortpathEgress'), createArray(createObject('name', 'Allow-Shortpath-STUN-Out', 'properties', createObject('description', 'RDP Shortpath Public networks: outbound UDP to STUN/TURN (3478) on Internet.', 'priority', 300, 'direction', 'Outbound', 'access', 'Allow', 'protocol', 'Udp', 'sourceAddressPrefix', '*', 'sourcePortRange', '*', 'destinationAddressPrefix', 'Internet', 'destinationPortRanges', createArray('3478', '3479')))), createArray()))]

properties.subnets: [concat(createArray(createObject('name', 'snet-hosts', 'properties', createObject('addressPrefix', parameters('hostsSubnetCidr'), 'networkSecurityGroup', createObject('id', resourceId('Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups', variables('nsgHostsName'))), 'privateEndpointNetworkPolicies', 'Enabled')), createObject('name', 'snet-pe', 'properties', createObject('addressPrefix', parameters('peSubnetCidr'), 'networkSecurityGroup', createObject('id', resourceId('Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups', variables('nsgPeName'))), 'privateEndpointNetworkPolicies', 'Disabled'))), if(variables('deployMgmtSubnet'), createArray(createObject('name', 'snet-mgmt', 'properties', createObject('addressPrefix', parameters('mgmtSubnetCidr'), 'networkSecurityGroup', createObject('id', resourceId('Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups', variables('nsgMgmtName'))), 'privateEndpointNetworkPolicies', 'Enabled'))), createArray()))]

metadata.description: Registration-token expiration. Default = utcNow + 14 days (computed at deploy time).

schedules.name: weekdays
schedules.rampDownNotificationMessage: Your session will be logged off in 30 minutes.

$fxv#0.title: Secured-AVD marketplace image alias map
$fxv#0.description: Canonical alias-to-imageReference map. The 'alias' key (e.g. 'win11-24h2-avd-m365') is what users pass via the imageReference variable. Image SKUs are pinned to 'latest' for floor-version freshness; pin to a specific version in production via the version key.

win11-24h2-avd-m365.displayName: Windows 11 Enterprise multi-session, version 24H2 + Microsoft 365 Apps
win11-24h2-avd-m365.recommendedFor: Pooled host pools with Office 365 ProPlus users (most common AVD profile)

win11-24h2-avd.recommendedFor: Pooled host pools without bundled M365 (BYO Office or no Office)

win11-23h2-avd-m365.displayName: Windows 11 Enterprise multi-session, version 23H2 + Microsoft 365 Apps
win11-23h2-avd-m365.recommendedFor: Fleets pinned to 23H2 until 24H2 burn-in complete

win10-22h2-avd-m365.recommendedFor: Legacy Windows 10 fleets (Win10 EOL Oct 2025 — plan migration)

shortpathScriptMap.Managed: New-Item -Path "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services" -Force | Out-Null; Set-ItemProperty -Path "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services" -Name fUseUdpPortRedirector -Value 1 -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services" -Name UdpPortNumber -Value 3390 -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services" -Name ICEControl -Value 1 -Force; New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName "AVD Shortpath UDP 3390" -Direction Inbound -Protocol UDP -LocalPort 3390 -Action Allow -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
shortpathScriptMap.Public: New-Item -Path "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services" -Force | Out-Null; Set-ItemProperty -Path "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services" -Name fUseUdpPortRedirector -Value 1 -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services" -Name ICEControl -Value 0 -Force
shortpathScriptMap.Both: New-Item -Path "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services" -Force | Out-Null; Set-ItemProperty -Path "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services" -Name fUseUdpPortRedirector -Value 1 -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services" -Name UdpPortNumber -Value 3390 -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services" -Name ICEControl -Value 2 -Force; New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName "AVD Shortpath UDP 3390" -Direction Inbound -Protocol UDP -LocalPort 3390 -Action Allow -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

resources.name: [format('{0}/{1}', format('{0}{1}', parameters('vmNamePrefix'), padLeft(string(range(0, parameters('vmCount'))[range(0, parameters('vmCount'))[copyIndex()]]), 2, '0')), 'ShortpathConfig')]
dependsOn: [resourceId('Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/extensions', format('{0}{1}', parameters('vmNamePrefix'), padLeft(string(range(0, parameters('vmCount'))[range(0, parameters('vmCount'))[range(0, parameters('vmCount'))[copyIndex()]]]), 2, '0')), 'AADLoginForWindows')]

resources.name: [format('{0}/{1}', format('{0}{1}', parameters('vmNamePrefix'), padLeft(string(range(0, parameters('vmCount'))[range(0, parameters('vmCount'))[copyIndex()]]), 2, '0')), 'AVD-DSC')]
dependsOn: [resourceId('Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/extensions', format('{0}{1}', parameters('vmNamePrefix'), padLeft(string(range(0, parameters('vmCount'))[range(0, parameters('vmCount'))[range(0, parameters('vmCount'))[copyIndex()]]]), 2, '0')), 'ShortpathConfig')]

resources.name: [guid(resourceId('Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines', format('{0}{1}', parameters('vmNamePrefix'), padLeft(string(range(0, parameters('vmCount'))[range(0, parameters('vmCount'))[copyIndex()]]), 2, '0'))), parameters('desktopUserGroupObjectId'), 'VirtualMachineUserLogin')]

[guid(resourceId('Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines', format('{0}{1}', parameters('vmNamePrefix'), padLeft(string(range(0, parameters('vmCount'))[range(0, parameters('vmCount'))[copyIndex()]]), 2, '0'))), parameters('adminUserGroupObjectId'), 'VirtualMachineAdministratorLogin')]

drainOldHostPoolPowerShellSnippet.value:
[if(parameters('drainOldHostPool'), '# === DRAIN OLD HOST POOL ===========================================================
# This sets allowNewSession = $false on every existing session host in the OLD host
# pool, blocking new connections while letting current sessions disconnect on their
# own schedule. Existing users stay productive; new connections route to the NEW
# stack once you publish the new workspace to them.
Connect-AzAccount -SubscriptionId ''${oldHpSubId}''
Get-AzWvdSessionHost -ResourceGroupName ''${oldHpRg}'' -HostPoolName ''${oldHpName}'' \
  | ForEach-Object {
      $name = $_.Name.Split(''/'')[-1]
      Write-Host "Draining $name"
      Update-AzWvdSessionHost -ResourceGroupName ''${oldHpRg}'' -HostPoolName ''${oldHpName}'' \
        -Name $name -AllowNewSession:$false
    }
# ===================================================================================
', '')]

value: [format('1. Confirm NEW stack health: connect to {0} from a test user account, validate desktop launches and RDP Shortpath engages.', variables('n').workspace)]
[format('2. (Recommended) Add {0} to user assignments WITHOUT removing the old workspace — users will see both during overlap window.', variables('n').workspace)]
[format('3. Drain OLD host pool {0} using the PowerShell snippet above.', variables('oldHpName'))]

[format('4. Monitor old host pool session count via Log Analytics: AVDConnections | where _ResourceId =~ "{0}" | summarize ConnectedUsers=dcount(UserName) by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m).', parameters('existingHostPoolResourceId'))]
[format('5. When connected users = 0 for N hours, remove {0} from user assignments.', variables('oldWsName'))]
[format('6. Decommission: delete {0}, {1}, and old session host VMs. Keep diagnostic logs for audit.', variables('oldHpName'), variables('oldWsName'))]
